speaker: MATT
raw_text: Thank you for the punctuation. Upon gathering your supplies after a night's rest, you managed to, through the trade of a bottle of wine, get passage across the lake onto the central island, which at one time housed the abode of a witch that had long collapsed. Waiting for nightfall, you approached the center of the grove that existed there on the island and found near this ruin this same fiendish nergaliid, as well as Toya, the 12-year-old dwarf girl who had been affixed to him for the performances in the carnival. You did battle with this large fiendish entity who summoned a cluster of imps and managed to knock a few of you pretty heavily to the ground before you finished it off. We left off as you all were finishing licking your wounds, catching your breath, and now as the moonlit night gets its darkest in the sky, you figure out what the next step is in gathering means of absolving yourselves of suspicion. As you've all now taken a moment to yourselves, you can hear the sound of the nearby crickets getting more and more deafening as the silence crawls over the center of the lake. Toya, the young girl, still lies unconscious. The remains of the devil toad, still partially burnt from the final blow delivered by Fjord, are mostly intact above its shoulders, head, and its legs. What would you like to do?

speaker: MATT
raw_text: Okay. It's a tough form. Not only is the exterior of its flesh blubbery and a heavy rubber-like substance that is glistening with whatever mucus-like liquid it naturally produces, but then beneath it, the muscle sinew is extremely tough. It takes you the better part of 20 minutes to finally get the head entirely off the rest of the torso. It weighs enough to the point where you're going to need one or two other hands to get it anywhere.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: The whole body.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: The whole body with all of you could probably fit. It doesn't have the usual-- you could probably fit it.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: How about just the head?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: The head it would probably do, but the full body, I'd have to make a few rolls.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: I'm going to give the body a quick search to see if there's anything.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: You already did.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Yeah, I got a bunch of rings. See?

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Do I recognize any of these rings?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: No, you do not.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: All right. That's disturbing.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: I got one, too.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Has the head been damaged at all, or is it mostly intact?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: It's mostly intact. There are a few gashes on it from the general fray, but for the most part it's still a solid head.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Molly, you've got them impressive swords. You want to do some hacking?

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Suppose I should.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: I'd say you have a pretty impressive sword, too.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: I'm going to try and carve off the head.

speaker: MATT
raw_text: She leans into you, Molly. After a few minutes, she falls asleep against you.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: It might do for you to apologize.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Sorry.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Sorry for what, Beau?

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Really, Jester?

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Really.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: No, I want to hear this too.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Sorry for choking you with my stick.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Somehow that was better than what I was hoping for.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Rated T for Teen.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: And I'll never do it again? (laughter)

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: And I'll never do it again. As long as you don't try that again, of course, I guess.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: It was only because Kylre was trying to kill us and she was afraid that you were helping him.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Yeah, I wasn't trying to hurt you. I was just trying to get you to stop singing. But it was so abrasive.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: You know how people outside can be. The mean girl was just afraid of you and we're going to let her go on this one.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "I'm tired."

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Yeah, I know. Let's get you home, okay?

speaker: MATT
raw_text: There's no boats at the dock that are being manned currently. There are a few boats out on the water that you saw in the distance. There are some night fishermen. There's nobody immediately in the vicinity.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: It was only because Kylre was trying to kill us and she was afraid that you were helping him.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Yeah, I wasn't trying to hurt you. I was just trying to get you to stop singing. But it was so abrasive.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: You know how people outside can be. The mean girl was just afraid of you and we're going to let her go on this one.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "I'm tired."

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Yeah, I know. Let's get you home, okay?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: She leans into you, Molly. After a few minutes, she falls asleep against you.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: That went well, I think.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Choked you with my stick?!

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: I did.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: She apologized for exactly what she did.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: That was certainly memorable.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "All right, we're here." The boat hits the edge of the dock. He hops off and pulls the rope up. Slowly manages the boat over to the side of the dock, ties it up.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: How many people are around the dock right now?

speaker: MATT
raw_text: To get across, I would say I'd allow a short rest.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: I immediately drop to my knees and start looking for a shoe.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: What, what?

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Did you say a shoe?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: I would like for you to make a-- (laughs)

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: You turned into Mr. Rogers?

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: A shoe!?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: I would like for you to make a deception check, with disadvantage please.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: My shoe!

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Eight.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: As Beau's on the ground frantically searching for a shoe and Nott's there in the darkness, the guard with his arms crossed goes (whistles). He begins to walk and approach you as two more guards close off the other side of the alley and they begin to come up to you guys.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: How far away are they from me? Too far?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Too far.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Drop one of those rings, drop a ring!

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: It's not a shoe! I drop a ring. Wait, did we take a short rest when we were on the boat? We did. It was a short rest on the boat.

speaker: MATT
raw_text: You guys slowly make your way through the low grass over towards where the outside of the carnival is. As you approach, you can see there's about five crownsguard that are making rounds around the outside. It looks like where previously they had been keeping watch over the people that were there, since the murder of two of their own, it has heightened the intensity of the watch. It looks like it's fairly well watched. You get the sense, at a distance, that you would have a hard time getting in there. You can try and sneak her in and sneak out, but it seems dangerous.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: This is the stupidest thing!

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: So stupid!

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Level two!

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Do I have to go, too?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Oh yeah.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: But I was just a bystander! I was walking down here and I saw these two ruffians and I was going to go get help.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Make a deception check.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: It's actually true, she wasn't with us.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Zero. (laughter)

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: You rolled a zero?

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Look at him!

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: What's your modifier, negative two?

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Three minus three is zero.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: That went into my nose.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Brave new world.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: As you're telling him this, your mask goes (creak). He goes, "Arrest that creature!"

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: We're going to die from bad paperwork.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Yeah, we really are.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Literally, we're going to die from bad paperwork.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: You both are put in chains. You're put in chains both arms and legs.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Oh, that's just mean.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Racism!

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: They bring you over to the stockade. Mollymauk. I need you to make a stealth check with advantage.

speaker: MATT
raw_text: "This whole thing can be solved first thing in the morning. I'll let you sleep it off. Have some water. Come back when you're feeling a little more yourself."

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Make an intimidation check.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Nice.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Oh, come on.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Just keep it in the--

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: I wish I had gone with that because it was great there but it's not great here. That is a nine!

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: It is indeed. He sits there and glares down at you for a second, your posturing, this filthy, scruffy man in a coat that wandered up out of nowhere covered in a little bit of his own blood. He just looks down at you for a second, "Sorry. She's done for the night."

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: They keep regular hours here.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Excuse me. There are very good people being held under house arrest in a tavern right now. They are innocent. I have evidence that I can show her. This whole thing can be solved now.

speaker: MATT
raw_text: She goes, (sighs) "Hold onto them for just a moment." She walks back inside. A moment passes, and she returns with four other guards.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Help. Help. Help. (laughter)

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Funny story. Yes, these are the people I was talking about. Nothing has changed, however, and I am only asking to take another ten minutes of your time to walk to the dock. One of the performers has been killed this evening, and I have done it. I staked out the circus because my friends were in trouble, and wrongly accused, and I knew this. So I staked out the circus this evening, and watched as this toad thing left the grounds there. It did this to me, and I, in a rage, followed it and destroyed it, and I will show you the remains of my work. We'll put it to bed. I'm either a clown or I'm a toad-killer. Let's see what it is.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Make a persuasion check.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Come on, you fucker.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Oh, goddamn.

speaker: MATT
raw_text: It has been close to an hour at this point.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Make a persuasion check.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Come on, you fucker.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Oh, goddamn.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: She goes, (sighs) "Hold onto them for just a moment." She walks back inside. A moment passes, and she returns with four other guards.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: I keep doing this so I can make a jingling sound with my chains.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: You know, that's probably agitating the issue. Let's go to the docks.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: She comes up. "All right. Drag them along with us. Show us what this thing is, and if what you say is not true, you'll join them. How's that sound?"

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: I'm good with these terms. Follow me please.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "All right."

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: You know, the only reason we were out of the inn is because we were looking for you, Caleb. We didn't know where you went.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: I am not going to answer her. I'm just walking very firmly, with purpose, to the docks.

speaker: MATT
raw_text: All right, make a stealth check, please. You do not have advantage anymore, that has passed. It's been more than a hour.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Who is it?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "Oh, they will be here shortly."

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: I sit down on a crate and I stop talking.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Okay, and this awkward period of time with the guards all just standing there, holding you as you wait patiently. Some of them are tapping their fingers. You start rattling your chains and two guards pull the chains taut so you can't do it anymore. Mollymauk, as you begin to make your way back into town, because you're making your way through the dock district along the lakeside and you look up ahead, and you can see there's a couple of torches being held by crownsguard right where the head was left, and you can see Caleb, and then three of your allies in chains.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Hmm. (sighs)

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Oh no! What are you going to do?

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: I'm going to get a little closer.

speaker: MATT
raw_text: At this point the guard that had left earlier, who has been gone for about 15 to 20 minutes or so, comes back with a brisk jog. There's another figure walking up behind him, also jogging. You can see light gray hood and cloak around and as the figure approaches into the torch light, pulls back the hood, and you see this long shoulder length curled blond hair. A very very delicate looking elven male, who approaches. "I believe I've been summoned, Norda?" "Yes sorry, this is Archivist Zeenoth. He's been in town for a few days and he comes from the library that knows a lot about a lot of things. I hope that he can help us in this. Zeenoth, they're calling this thing a fiend. What do you know about this?" He steps closer, kneels down next to the head.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Oh, what's the play? What's the play? Ah! I'm going to-- Yeah, fuck it. I'm going to walk up.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: All right.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: With briskly. There I find you! I've been looking everywhere. You're not supposed to leave the bloody inn you-- oh. (sighs) They're already in trouble, apparently.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "I love when they do this! Chain him up." You are shackled as well.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: All right. Less questions to answer.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: At this point, Norda is starting to smile a bit. She's like "Man, if we could have more criminals like you lot, my job would be far easier. I would get home a lot earlier."

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: We're not criminals! We're not! Me and Caleb were just travelling ever since we broke out of that-- Nothing!

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Dance number.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: No, it's good that you try so hard to protect the rights of giant frog creatures. I'm very impressed.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Fiend creatures specifically.

speaker: MATT
raw_text: At this point the guard that had left earlier, who has been gone for about 15 to 20 minutes or so, comes back with a brisk jog. There's another figure walking up behind him, also jogging. You can see light gray hood and cloak around and as the figure approaches into the torch light, pulls back the hood, and you see this long shoulder length curled blond hair. A very very delicate looking elven male, who approaches. "I believe I've been summoned, Norda?" "Yes sorry, this is Archivist Zeenoth. He's been in town for a few days and he comes from the library that knows a lot about a lot of things. I hope that he can help us in this. Zeenoth, they're calling this thing a fiend. What do you know about this?" He steps closer, kneels down next to the head.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Oh, what's the play? What's the play? Ah! I'm going to-- Yeah, fuck it. I'm going to walk up.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: All right.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: With briskly. There I find you! I've been looking everywhere. You're not supposed to leave the bloody inn you-- oh. (sighs) They're already in trouble, apparently.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "I love when they do this! Chain him up." You are shackled as well.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: All right. Less questions to answer.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: At this point, Norda is starting to smile a bit. She's like "Man, if we could have more criminals like you lot, my job would be far easier. I would get home a lot earlier."

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: We're not criminals! We're not! Me and Caleb were just travelling ever since we broke out of that-- Nothing!

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Dance number.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: No, it's good that you try so hard to protect the rights of giant frog creatures. I'm very impressed.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Fiend creatures specifically.

speaker: MATT
raw_text: "Yes, these creatures do frequent Xhorhas." He gives some look over, pulls a small book out of his side pouch. Begins to thumb through it, compares a few things. "I'm not a specialist, unfortunately. This creature is very much a very dangerous entity from the far east. I'm surprised it made it this far into civilization before being destroyed. It's a good thing it has been." He now gets to look at everyone, over the crew and goes, "Are you the ones who slew it?"

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: At this point the guard that had left earlier, who has been gone for about 15 to 20 minutes or so, comes back with a brisk jog. There's another figure walking up behind him, also jogging. You can see light gray hood and cloak around and as the figure approaches into the torch light, pulls back the hood, and you see this long shoulder length curled blond hair. A very very delicate looking elven male, who approaches. "I believe I've been summoned, Norda?" "Yes sorry, this is Archivist Zeenoth. He's been in town for a few days and he comes from the library that knows a lot about a lot of things. I hope that he can help us in this. Zeenoth, they're calling this thing a fiend. What do you know about this?" He steps closer, kneels down next to the head.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Xhorhas.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Hmm?

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: I just whisper Xhorhas.

speaker: MATT
raw_text: The crownsguard have pulled away and now it's just you guys and Bosun and Gustav now, out in the air and Gustav is rubbing his wrists. "Well, thank you everyone for helping. That could have been a lot worse. And, you know, it's fine. I know it's a lot of money, but shouldn't take me more than a few years. It's a nice town! Trostenwald is a nice town!"

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: How long have you been in there?

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: Immediately after I ran away. They sent out horses. I'm not that fast, but I did fuck up a few of the guards. "By accident. "

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: As you say that, you hear a couple (clears throat) and two of the crownsguard visibly bruised that are escorting you guys out into the night air before they eventually leave you to your own devices.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Molly, what do you think this means for the carnival?

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: I don't know. That is a question.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: I mean, if Gustav has to stay here then who's going to run it?

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: That's a lot of gold. That's like a life sentence worth of gold.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Well, I mean--

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Gold comes, gold goes.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: How much do you think the circus has?

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: I don't know how much the circus has and, honestly, I think Ornna would lie about it if we asked.

speaker: MATT
raw_text: "We'll come meet you at the Nestled Nook and I'll get a round for you all. As a thank you."

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Make a perception check.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Oh, god damn it, it was almost better. It was almost good. Ten.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Ten? You don't see anybody immediately around.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Was that Archivist Zeenoth that was outside?

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Archivist.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: That's what I said. Was that who it was that was like--

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: No, Zeenoth was still inside the courtroom when you were leaving.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Yeah. Different guy.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Oh, who was it that was outside?

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: We don't know, we didn't see him.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: It's very late, right?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Yeah, it's post-midnight now.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: I'm so tired.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Don't suppose you want to retire for the evening? Share a drink together now that this is somewhat behind us?

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: I will definitely drink. I think we should probably drink at our camp then. I'm fine inviting them.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "Tell you what. As a thanks, we'll grab Toya. We'll meet you guys-- where are you all staying?"

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: The Nestled Nook.

speaker: MATT
raw_text: As you make your way back to the Nestled Nook Inn, within the tavern, and within the next hour or so, Gustav and Bosun, Toya and Ornna all come in and join you. You guys crowd a few tables together, and under what is generally a pretty empty tavern this night, you guys, for the most part, dominate the center space.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: He was fine for so long. There was nothing for so long. Why? Things don't just...

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: I still think there's something creepy about this lake, man.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Maybe?

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: It gives me the heebie jeebies.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Everything gives me the heebie jeebies.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Well, if it is this thing that we've read about in the book, then perhaps he needed to feed more or was impatient for some reason?

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: What's happened with Toya?

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Shite.

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: How is she?

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: She is not good. She is taking it as you would expect.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Also Beau crushed her windpipe.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: That's true. With her stick.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: And, I'm sorry, are we also taking the bouncer into our little coven of weirdos. Is that a thing that's happening?

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: Oh, well--

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Always.

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: I mean, I kind of go on my own, but--

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Yeah, she's one of us.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: She's the charm, she has to come!

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: I'm too tired to argue. Let's get that drink.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: See, charm. Drink.

speaker: MATT
raw_text: The rest of the carnival in presence, Gustav goes and orders a round of drinks to all of you and then sits down. Toya still looks a bit shaken, but seems to be comforted with the rest of the family around her, that still remain at least. Desmond's come and joined as well. As you're all sitting together for a bit, there's a moment of quiet as you're all sipping, and just tiredly thinking over the events of the evening. Toya breaks the silence, "Is the family going away? Am I going to be alone again?" Ornna looks over at her and goes, "Not if I can help it. It's you and me, kid, against the world." Bosun pats Ornna on the shoulder and says, "Well, if you'd have me, you can probably use a strong arm." Ornna looks back at him. Ornna and him have had this rivalry tension for a while. She goes, "You know what? Sure. The trio makes for a strong pack of performers. Desmond, are you in?" And Desmond is just sitting there, staring into his cup and goes, "I see a new family before me. And one I fear that's a story I could only spoil. No, I thank you, but I'll find my own path." And he just sits there kind of quietly in this somewhat sad space. He gives a brief pat and side hug to Bosun before standing up and leaving. Gustav stands up and looks around. "I'm sorry for everything. For all the pain this has caused you, for all the pain it's caused you." Looks at Toya, "For all the pain it's caused you, little one."

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Looks could be deceiving.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Arm wrestle! Bets on Yasha.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Do it! Arm wrestle!

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: I just thought it would be a nice gesture.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: To arm wrestle?

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: No, to give up her sword.

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: What about your sword?

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Oh, I'm a paying patron here. They know me quite well.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: So? You've got to be putting something on the table, though. Well, if you want to surrender the sword or not surrender the sword.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Did you want to stay in that room any longer, Molly?

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: (laughs) Never.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: I don't know what we're talking about anymore.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: I don't either.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Let's get a drink.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Are you guys going to arm wrestle?

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: No.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Okay. How about "Boulder, Paper, Shears"? Wait, what is it? "Boulder, Parchment, Shears"!

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: That's a stupid game.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: I never heard of that game.

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: I think it's something that kids play. I have a better version of that game. We can play it later.

speaker: MATT
raw_text: Is a twelve year old girl! (laughter)

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: They're always looking for good performers. You would do well.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "Hmm. Let's go ahead and make our way. That sounds like a nice change of pace, and this empire is getting a little stuffy for me anyway."

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: I've bend down to Toya, and I take off one of the little jade bangles around my wrist, and I say, "Here. This will keep you safe from assholes named Beau. Except for that asshole, named Bo. He's a good Bo, but all other assholes named Beau."

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: You watch as Ornna elbows him and is like, "Oh, no, he's an asshole too. It's fine."

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: It's true.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Sorry. Sorry again about that.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: She takes it and goes, "It's okay. I know you did what you had to. But when I'm older, I'm going to get you back."

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: You better.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Your first nemesis!

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: My first nemesis!

speaker: MATT
raw_text: "It is only because it's been so difficult to find you. I had to call in a favor. Please, come with us. We have a warehouse."

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "Right, that'll probably be in the Hillsward. It's a bit pricey, but I don't know if it's open this late."

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: I know, but how much are we talking about?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "Oh, I'd put it maybe somewhere in the neighborhood of five silver for a bath."

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: That's expensive.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "But you get bathed like proper, multiple people bathing you."

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Northward, you say?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "No, Hillsward."

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Okay, good.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "I mean, no offense to you. Their clientele is a bit, upper class."

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: I'm not worried.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "Alright, fair enough."

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Danke. And I head up there.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Okay. Beau, as you exit with Zeenoth. As soon as you step into the night air, a second figure flanks your other side. The hooded figure from earlier that had caught you.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: I just look at Zeenoth and go really? Is this really necessary?

speaker: MATT
raw_text: This pain surges through the side of your temple, and travels through your spine, to all the points she had hit earlier, and your whole body tenses and seizes for a second. Your vision blurs, and your mouth stammers, and you're stunned for the next round. As she lands behind you, and walks around to the front of you, you're unable to, you can't move, you can't speak. "I can start you on this path. You can return to your wanderlust, your allies. Our kind learns more in the world than in a library. Agree to my instruction, to use the skills I teach you, as you return throughout your journeys. One day, you too, can become an expositor." You begin to regain control of your senses, and your speech begins to slowly return to you.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Correct, what the dodge action does is it means any attacks against you have disadvantage.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Oh. Cool.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: The first one did hit. Second one does not. The third one does. Two hits still get you. Both one point of bludgeoning damage. The knee comes down, and right as it cracks into your head, she shifts to the side and glides off, and as soon as her body glides off, her back is to yours, and her spinning elbow goes and cracks you in the side of the head, but it stops short, just barely hitting you. She's toying with you at this point.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: You said two more points?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Yes, two more points of bludgeoning damage. Make another constitution saving throw, please.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: 11. No, sorry, 12.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: This time, opposed to the pressure points being hit, there is the one smack on the side of your head.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Yeah?

speaker: MATT
raw_text: "Now, depending on what you wish to learn, there are different places where you can pull the information from the body." Over the next few hours, working with you, and over time--

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Okay. I got to hand it to you, that was awesome, and I want to know how to do that. You beat me.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "Very well. Your first lesson begins now."

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Okay. (groan)

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: She snaps her fingers, and you watch as Zeenoth steps back in, sheepishly. "Is it over, my dear? Is it done?" "I need you to stand here for a second, Zeenoth. I need a body. Let me show you the proper pressure points." Zeenoth's eyes go wide for a second. "I don't believe--" "You owe me a favor. I found her for you, did I not?" Zeenoth sighs, sags his head, and takes his cloak off.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: I grab that stool, and I drag it over.

speaker: MATT
raw_text: "I'll return to Zadash in the morning. Take what I have taught you. When you feel it's time for more tutelage, return to me. Do not worry. I have an eye on you."

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: I grab that stool, and I drag it over.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "Now, depending on what you wish to learn, there are different places where you can pull the information from the body." Over the next few hours, working with you, and over time--

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Does she beat the shit out of Zeenoth?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: You both do.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Sorry, Zeenoth. Sorry. God, we're such assholes.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "Assholes that the world needs, from time to time."

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Yeah. The world does need an asshole. We'll work on that one together. We'll work on that. That's good, that's good.

speaker: MATT
raw_text: "Perhaps. Who knows. Maybe one day you'll find the corruption inside our own."

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: What do I do until then, do I just continue--

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "Do what you must. Travel the world. Use what I've taught you. Learn about injustice. Those you don't trust, follow them. Bend them. Make them tell the truth and admit their guilt. Find where ever corruption is. Root it out and let the system do its job. If the system isn't doing its job, destroy the system."

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Why are you doing this? Why me?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "Because a long time ago, I was a wayward girl with no direction, was angry, and fighting at the world. And someone did this for me."

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: I don't really believe in systems or being a part of them, let alone a system that takes down other systems. Seems almost hypocritical.

speaker: MATT
raw_text: As you all come to consciousness in the morning, little sore, but recovered--mostly--from the battle that wrecked you the evening before. The fresh smells of cooked meats, and the sounds of various patrons below in the inn tavern floor, rouse you to the morning. What do you wish to do?

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: I don't really believe in systems or being a part of them, let alone a system that takes down other systems. Seems almost hypocritical.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "Perhaps. Who knows. Maybe one day you'll find the corruption inside our own."

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Touche.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "Go. Get some rest. Clean up a bit, I think you could use it."

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: You might have broke my wrist.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "It'll heal. Zeenoth." She slaps him awake. His unconscious body on the ground.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Bye, Zeenoth! Zeenoth!

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: (coughing)

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Zeenoth!

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "Oh dear."

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Bye.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "Goodbye. I have such great pleasure in saying goodbye, and good riddance."

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: I leave.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: You make your way back to the tavern, go back into the room, and, exhaustedly, pass out. Let's bring everyone else back in.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Hi, friends. How's it going?

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Are you alive?

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: I'm sure everything went according to plan.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Quiet dignity and grace.

speaker: MATT
raw_text: "For two hours, that will run-- for two baths adjoining, we'll put it at eight silver pieces."

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Yes, one moment. (quietly) Stay close to me and let me do the talking. (louder) Hello, please, don't focus-- my daughter is shy.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "I'm very sorry."

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: I've had a terrible day. Some disgusting derelict has vomited all over me, I smell like filth, and I would like to spend a couple of hours in your finest bath. Maybe not your finest. A good bath, for a couple of hours. My daughter and I. I will need adjoining baths, if you have that. For modesty's sake, my daughter and I.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "That can be done. Of course." Make a persuasion check.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: (humming) There's a 12.

speaker: MATT
raw_text: You are, actually. There is one incomplete arcane inscription in there. Unfortunately, it's been too burned, beyond recognition. You can see this was a spellbook. Mostly destroyed, but there are two complete spells in there that, if you wish to, you could transcribe over into your book. Those would be Arcane Lock and Unseen Servant.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: While you bathe?

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Yes.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: You'll still be the same Caleb when you're done, right?

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Yes, I will be the same Caleb.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: I've just gotten used to you, and now you're going to change a bit.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Trust me, I will not.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: All right.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Once she is clear of the wall, I'm going to start actually disrobing, although I guess the illusion stays.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: The illusion stays. Strangely, clothes start apparating out of you. (slurping)

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: It's like a bad video game glitch. Bad texture glitch.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Nott, I'm probably going to go quiet for a little while.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: No problem. I'll just be over here, you know, bathing.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Enjoy it. I find the second book and examine it. Am I able to read this?

speaker: MATT
raw_text: With nary a squeak you completely rearrange in a period of about two and a half minutes most of the price cards that are dangling from a number of the tools, their placement. You guys all sit back and watch as this glorious display-- you can almost hear the William Tell Overture begin to play in the back of your head as she rummages back and forth throughout the interior of the chamber, turning things around and sticking them in the ground. It has been completely rearranged.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: We are looking for pickles, did you get any yet?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "No, we don't sell pickles here, sorry."

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Most general stores have pickles, this is the only thing.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: You're not wrong, generally, yeah.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "We can order some pickles, if you like."

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Yeah, you probably should. I think you would do well if you sold pickles. Do you have maps for sale of the area?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "The area? Let me check. I'll be right back."

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Does she leave the room?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Yeah, she does. She leaves the room.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Don't-- put your hands in your pockets.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Don't put my hands in my pockets, got it! I start running around and just moving everything on the shelves again the same way as before.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Make a sleight of hand check.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: You're just moving things?

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Yeah, just like moving--

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Please be Laura when she was five and break shit.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Oh yes! 18 plus five.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: This is beautiful.

speaker: MATT
raw_text: It'll be about the same pace as on foot. At a fast pace on foot, you guys can traverse about 30 miles a day. That also means you're moving at a fast pace, so you're not being completely aware of your surroundings, you have a penalty to passively seeing things around you. But you get there quicker.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: I'll take it.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Unless you're a foreigner, everyone knows about Rexxentrum.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Yes, it's huge. For player comparison, it's twice the size of Emon. It's a massive city.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Zadash?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: No, not Zadash, Rexxentrum.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Zadash is medium, or smaller.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Okay. Yeah.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: How far did that map say, when I looked at it? 200 miles you said to Zadash?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: To Zadash, roughly 200 miles or so, if you're taking the road, quicker if you take a straight line, but you're not as safe.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Well, I think it's good.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Are we going to walk there? Should we get horses?

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: We have a horse.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: How many horses?

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: We have a horse.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: A horse and a carriage. I'm assuming it can't fit all of us.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Yeah, like a wagon.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: We have a wagon.
